docs.title: JPI - JAX Parallel Interface
docs.text: JPI provides MPI-based parallel operations for JAX, enabling distributed computing with JAX arrays across multiple processes.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
JPI offers a collection of collective communication operations optimized for JAX:

  • allgather: Gather arrays from all processes
  • allreduce: Reduce arrays across all processes
  • bcast: Broadcast arrays from one process to all others
  • barrier: Synchronize all processes

All operations are designed to work seamlessly with JAX's compilation and differentiation systems.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• JAX Integration: Full compatibility with jax.jit, jax.grad, and other JAX transformations
  • FFI Backend: Built on MPI for high-performance distributed computing

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
import jax
import jax.numpy as jnp
from mpi4py import MPI
from jpi.interface import allreduce, bcast
from jpi.interface.token import make_token

# Create some data
x = jnp.array([1.0, 2.0, 3.0])
token = gen_token()

# Broadcast from rank 0
result, token = bcast(x, token, root=0)

# Sum across all processes
sum_result, token = allreduce(x, token, op=MPI.SUM)

docs.title: gen_token()
docs.text:
Generate a synchronization token for MPI operations.

Returns:

Type Description

A scalar JAX array that serves as a synchronization token.

Note

Tokens should be threaded through MPI operations to maintain proper ordering. Each MPI operation consumes a token and produces a new one.

Example 
from jpi.interface.token import gen_token
from jpi.interface import bcast, allreduce

# Create initial token
token = gen_token()

# Thread token through operations
result1, token = bcast(data1, token, root=0)
result2, token = allreduce(data2, token, MPI.SUM)

docs.title: gen_token()
docs.text:
Generate a synchronization token for MPI operations.

Returns:

Type Description

A scalar JAX array that serves as a synchronization token.

Note

Tokens should be threaded through MPI operations to maintain proper ordering. Each MPI operation consumes a token and produces a new one.

Example 
from jpi.interface.token import gen_token
from jpi.interface import bcast, allreduce

# Create initial token
token = gen_token()

# Thread token through operations
result1, token = bcast(data1, token, root=0)
result2, token = allreduce(data2, token, MPI.SUM)

docs.title: allgather(x, token, comm=None)
docs.text:
Gather arrays from all processes and distribute to all.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default x Array

Local array to contribute to the gather operation. Must have the same shape on all processes except possibly the first dimension.

required token Array

Synchronization token for ordering operations.

required comm

MPI communicator. If None, uses the default communicator.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description result

Concatenated array with shape (total_elements, *x.shape[1:]), where total_elements = sum of x.shape[0] across all processes.

new_token

Updated synchronization token.

Example 
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jpi.interface import allgather
from jpi.interface.token import gen_token

# Each rank contributes different data
local_data = jnp.array([rank, rank + 1])  # rank-specific data
token = gen_token()
result, token = allgather(local_data, token) # result contains data from all ranks concatenated

docs.title: allreduce(x, token, op, comm=None)
docs.text:
Perform a reduction operation across all processes.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default x Array

Local array to contribute to the reduction.

required token Array

Synchronization token for ordering operations.

required op

MPI reduction operation. Supported operations include: - MPI.SUM: Element-wise sum across all processes - MPI.PROD: Element-wise product across all processes - MPI.MAX: Element-wise maximum across all processes - MPI.MIN: Element-wise minimum across all processes

required comm

MPI communicator. If None, uses the default communicator.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description result

Array containing the reduction result (same on all processes).

new_token

Updated synchronization token.

Raises:

Type Description NotImplementedError

If the backward pass is not implemented for the specified reduction operation.

Example 
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jpi.interface import allreduce
from jpi.interface.token import gen_token
from mpi4py import MPI

# Sum arrays across all processes
local_data = jnp.array([1.0, 2.0]) * (rank + 1)
token = gen_token()
result, token = allreduce(local_data, token, MPI.SUM) # result contains the sum from all processes

docs.title: barrier(token, comm=None)
docs.text:
Synchronize all processes in the communicator.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default token Array

Synchronization token for ordering operations. The token is passed through unchanged but ensures proper sequencing.

required comm

MPI communicator. If None, uses the default communicator.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description new_token

Updated synchronization token (same value as input).

Example 
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jpi.interface import barrier
from jpi.interface.token import gen_token

# Ensure all processes reach this point before continuing
token = gen_token()
token = barrier(token) # Now all processes have synchronized

docs.title: bcast(x, token, root, comm=None)
docs.text:
Broadcast an array from one process to all others.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default x Array

Input array to broadcast. Only meaningful on the root process.

required token Array

Synchronization token for ordering operations.

required root

Rank of the root process that owns the data to broadcast.

required comm

MPI communicator. If None, uses the default communicator.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description result

The broadcasted array (same on all processes).

new_token

Updated synchronization token.

Example 
import jax.numpy as jnp
from jpi.interface import bcast
from jpi.interface.token import gen_token

# On rank 0: broadcast this data
if rank == 0:
    data = jnp.array([1.0, 2.0, 3.0])
else:
    data = jnp.zeros(3)  # Will be overwritten

token = gen_token()
result, token = bcast(data, token, root=0) # Now all processes have [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]

docs.title: gen_token()
docs.text:
Generate a synchronization token for MPI operations.

Returns:

Type Description

A scalar JAX array that serves as a synchronization token.

Note

Tokens should be threaded through MPI operations to maintain proper ordering. Each MPI operation consumes a token and produces a new one.

Example 
from jpi.interface.token import gen_token

# Create initial token

# Thread token through operations

result2, token = allreduce(data2, token, MPI.SUM)
